reasoning: For April 20, FIDE rankings only update officially on the 1st of each month and the Wikipedia page updates accordingly. Therefore, the list on April 20 will be identical to the April 13 list, ensuring So's rank remains 12 (0.95 probability). For May 13, the May 1st list will be active. So is currently at 2748, tied with Mamedyarov and 1 point ahead of Aronian. His rank is extremely sensitive to minor live rating changes. As a historically top-10 player, he has a decent chance to maintain or improve his rank <=12, but the tight competition pulls the probability down to 0.56. For July 12 and October 10, the greater distance into the future introduces wider variance in player ratings, compounded by the ascent of young players and Grand Chess Tour classical events over the summer. Therefore, the probability of him maintaining a top-12 position regresses closer to 0.5, reflecting increased uncertainty over the longer horizon.
